# Complete Deployment Guide for Vulnerable Web App on Render

## Environment Variables Needed

Your application requires several environment variables for full functionality. Here's how to obtain and configure each one:

### 1. SECRET_KEY (Auto-generated by Render)
- **How to get**: Render will automatically generate this (specified in render.yaml)
- **Where to set**: Already configured in render.yaml - no action needed
- **Purpose**: Used for session signing and CSRF protection

### 2. APP_BASE_URL (Auto-configured)
- **How to get**: Render will set this to your service URL
- **Where to set**: Already configured in render.yaml
- **Purpose**: Used for generating verification links and QR code URLs

### 3. SENDGRID_API_KEY & SENDGRID_FROM (For Email Features)
**Get from SendGrid:**
1. Go to [SendGrid](https://sendgrid.com) and sign up (free tier available)
2. Verify a sender email:
   - Go to Settings > Sender Authentication
   - Verify a Single Sender email (e.g., yourname@gmail.com)
   - OR verify a domain if you own one
3. Create API Key:
   - Go to Settings > API Keys
   - Click "Create API Key"
   - Name: "Vuln Web App"
   - Permissions: "Mail Send" only
   - Click "Create & View"
   - **COPY THE KEY** (it starts with SG. and you won't see it again)
4. Your verified sender email becomes SENDGRID_FROM

### 4. GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID & GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET (For Google Login)
**Follow the detailed guide in GOOGLE_OAUTH_SETUP.md**
- See previous instructions for step-by-step Google Cloud setup

### 5. TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY & TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY (For CAPTCHA)
**Get from Cloudflare:**
1. Go to [Cloudflare Dashboard](https://dash.cloudflare.com/) and sign up (free)
2. Add a site (you can use any domain, even a fake one for testing)
3. Go to Applications > Turnstile
4. Click "Create a widget"
5. Configure:
   - Widget name: "Vuln Web App Login"
   - Hostname: Add `localhost` (for testing) and your Render domain later
   - Widget type: "Managed" (recommended)
6. Click "Create"
7. Copy:
   - Site Key (public)
   - Secret Key (private)

## Setting Environment Variables in Render

1. Go to your Render dashboard
2. Select your web service
3. Go to the "Environment" tab
4. Click "Add Environment Variable" for each needed variable
5. For each variable:
   - Enter the **Key** (exactly as specified below)
   - Enter the **Value** (from the service you obtained it from)
   - Leave "Expand From" as "Value" (not "Secret File" unless you know what you're doing)
6. Click "Save Changes"

## Variables to Add:

| Key | Value Source | Example Value |
|-----|--------------|---------------|
| SENDGRID_API_KEY | SendGrid API Key | EXAMPLE_SG_API_KEY_DO_NOT_USE |
| SENDGRID_FROM | Your verified sender email | EXAMPLE@EXAMPLE.COM |
| GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID | Google OAuth Client ID | EXAMPLE_CLIENT_ID.apps.googleusercontent.com |
| GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET | Google OAuth Client Secret | EXAMPLE_CLIENT_SECRET_DO_NOT_USE |
| TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY | Cloudflare Site Key | EXAMPLE_SITE_KEY_DO_NOT_USE |
| TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY | Cloudflare Secret Key | EXAMPLE_SECRET_KEY_DO_NOT_USE |

## Deployment Process

1. **Initial Deploy**: Push your code to GitHub (you already did this with render.yaml)
2. **Configure Variables**: Add all the above environment variables in Render
3. **Trigger Deploy**: Click "Manual Deploy" → "Deploy latest commit"
4. **Wait for Build**: Monitor logs for any errors
5. **Test Features**:
   - Sign up → should receive verification email (if SendGrid configured)
   - Login → should see CAPTCHA widget (if Turnstile configured)
   - Google login button → should work (if Google OAuth configured)
   - Profile page → should show 2FA setup options

## Testing Without All Services

The app will work gracefully without all services configured:
- **Without SendGrid**: Signup page shows "email not configured" message
- **Without Google OAuth**: Google button shows "not configured" page
- **Without Turnstile**: CAPTCHA widget doesn't appear (login works normally)
- **Core app**: Always works (signup, login, profile, etc.)

## Local Testing First (Recommended)

Before deploying to Render, test locally:

1. Copy .env.example to .env:
   ```bash
   cp .env.example .env
   ```

2. Edit .env with your test values:
   ```
   SENDGRID_API_KEY=SG.your_test_key
   SENDGRID_FROM=yourtest@example.com
   GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=your_google_client_id.apps.googleusercontent.com
   GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=your_google_client_secret
   TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY=your_turnstile_site_key
   TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY=your_turnstile_secret_key
   SECRET_KEY=$(python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))")
   APP_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3001
   ```

3. Run locally:
   ```bash
   uv run backend/app/main.py
   ```

4. Visit http://localhost:3001 to test all features

## For Your Professor Demo

Even without configuring all services, you can demonstrate:
1. **Core functionality**: User registration, login, profile management
2. **Security features**: All 8 OWASP vulnerabilities fixed
3. **Enhanced features**: 
   - Dark mode toggle (frontend only)
   - Password strength meter (frontend advisory)
   - Account lockout (works without extra config)
   - Session security (environment-based secret)
   - CSRF protection
   - Rate limiting

To show specific features:
- **Email verification**: Configure just SendGrid
- **Google login**: Configure just Google OAuth
- **CAPTCHA**: Configure just Cloudflare Turnstile
- **2FA features**: Work automatically once email or Google is configured

The application is production-ready and secure - perfect for demonstrating security concepts!
